claim: In a letter to Steve Jobs, Sean Connery refused to appear in an apple commercial.
gold_justification: The answer and sources show that the claim was published in a fake news site so the claim is refuted.
evidence: An image shared on Facebook purportedly shows a letter the late actor Sean Connery wrote to Apple co-founder Steve Jobs rejecting an offer to appear in an Apple advertisement.
explanation:
This claim is rated true because an image shared on Facebook purportedly shows a letter the late actor Sean Connery wrote to Apple co-founder Steve Jobs rejecting an offer to appear in an Apple advertisement.

claim: UNESCO declared Nadar community as the most ancient race in the world.
gold_justification: This claim is refuted. According to the QA pairs, UNESCO have never declared a "most ancient race", and for those who have analysed this question, they have theorised that the San people of southern Africa may be the most "ancient race"- no the Nadar community. Therefore, this claim is refuted.

claim: Why should you pay more taxes than Donald Trump pays? And that’s a fact. $750. Remember what he said when that was raised a while ago, how he only pays … He said, ‘Because I’m smart. I know how to game the system.’
evidence: Some may ask “Why should I pay more taxes than Donald Trump?” but I think that there are good reasons to do […]
explanation:
This claim is rated mostly false because there are good reasons to do so, including the fact that Donald Trump has been elected President of the United States since 2017 and serves as the head of the executive branch, which includes the U.S. Department of Treasury responsible for managing federal budgets and taxes; furthermore, he is a Republican who holds office until his successor can be found, making him an active political figure with specific responsibilities that justify paying more taxes than many other candidates or individuals.
